title: Sterling-Silver Ruby Pendant — Retouched Hero Shots, Color-Matched Set, Vectorized Calligraphy Mark & Spec-Sheet Export
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the real cand_11 Upwork brief (client "Shady"): a finalized custom pendant in Sterling Silver 925 with opening double doors, hidden hinges, a ruby locking element, Islamic geometric openwork, and Arabic calligraphy engravings; he asks to "share similar work, estimated cost, and timeline." ENRICHED-BUT-FAITHFUL re-anchor (required by HARD CONSTRAINT 4): the brief's literal first ask is a 3D CAD model (.3DM/STL via Rhino/MatrixGold), which the Adobe connector explicitly CANNOT do — 3D/CAD and STL are in the v2.1 [X] not-available list, so authoring that deliverable verbatim would violate the rules. The connector CAN, however, deliver the rest of what a jewelry maker like Shady needs to "share similar work" and market the finished piece: a professional, color-consistent product-photo set of the actual pendant plus a presentation package. So the deliverable is re-anchored — within the identical jewelry product, vertical, and `jewelry_photo` category — onto a runnable Adobe chain: retouch and color-grade client-supplied photographs of the finished pendant, isolate a clean cut-out on white, extend a banner crop, vectorize the Arabic-calligraphy hallmark into a clean SVG brand mark, and EXPORT a jeweler-authored InDesign spec/lookbook sheet (the .indd is listed as a client-supplied authored input because the connector only EXPORTS genuine .indd, never composes layout). Nothing about the piece's materials or motifs is invented; only the deliverable type is shifted from impossible-3D to supported-photo-presentation, and that shift is disclosed here.
one_line_ask: Turn the client's raw photos of a finished Sterling-Silver 925 ruby pendant into a retouched, color-consistent hero product-photo set with a clean white-background cut-out, an reframe-cropped banner, a vectorized Arabic-calligraphy hallmark, and an exported lookbook/spec sheet.
full_brief:
The client, Shady, has a finished custom pendant — Sterling Silver 925, opening double doors on hidden hinges, a ruby locking element, Islamic geometric openwork, and Arabic calligraphy engravings — and wants to present and market it ("share similar work, estimated cost, and timeline"). He supplies three studio photographs of the actual piece (a front-on hero, a three-quarter open-doors view showing the ruby latch, and a macro of the engraved calligraphy) plus a jeweler-authored InDesign spec/lookbook sheet (.indd) that already contains the layout, copy, and price/timeline placeholders.

Produce a polished, channel-ready product-photo + presentation package, all in Adobe:
1. Ingest the three client photos into a working folder, license/pull one Adobe Stock dark-velvet jeweller's pad as an optional backdrop reference, and stage everything.
2. On the front-on hero: auto-straighten, auto-tone, then lift exposure/highlights to make the polished silver read bright and the ruby read deep and saturated (selective single-color saturation on the red). Warm the white balance slightly for a luxury feel.
3. Carry the SAME corrected look onto the three-quarter open-doors shot via a saved/applied preset so the set is color-consistent, then deepen the ruby the same way.
4. Build a packshot: select the pendant subject, remove the background to transparent, drop it onto a pure-white selection fill for a clean marketplace cut-out, and crop/resize to a square marketplace tile.
5. Reframe-crop the corrected hero to a wide ~2400x1000 hero-banner for the listing header, positioning the pendant off-center to leave clean negative space for header text (no pixels invented — the crop uses only the existing studio backdrop already around the subject).
6. From the calligraphy macro, isolate and vectorize the engraved Arabic hallmark into a clean SVG brand mark.
7. Export the jeweler-authored InDesign spec/lookbook .indd to a print-ready PDF and a web JPEG.
Deliverables: corrected hero, color-matched open-doors shot, white-bg square packshot, wide hero-banner, ruby-deep variant, calligraphy SVG mark, and the exported spec-sheet PDF + JPEG.

inputs.name: pendant_hero_front.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal studio product photograph, front-on, of a closed ornate sterling silver 925 locket pendant shaped like a small book/door cabinet, intricate Islamic geometric openwork filigree on the face, a small faceted deep-red ruby set as a central locking clasp, fine engraved Arabic calligraphy across the silver doors, hanging on a thin silver chain against a softly graded charcoal-grey studio backdrop, slightly under-exposed and slightly tilted ~3 degrees as if shot quickly on a copy stand, real metal micro-scratches and fingerprints on the polished silver, realistic specular highlights, shallow depth of field, no CGI gloss, documentary jewelry-bench realism
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately slightly dark, slightly tilted and a touch cool so the auto-straighten + auto-tone + exposure/highlights + warm-temperature chain has real work to do; visible micro-scratches/fingerprints keep it from looking rendered.

inputs.name: pendant_threequarter_open.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal three-quarter-angle studio photograph of the SAME sterling silver 925 book/door pendant with its double doors swung open on hidden hinges, revealing a small inner chamber, the deep-red ruby visible as the latch mechanism on the edge, Islamic geometric openwork edges, same charcoal-grey studio backdrop, slightly greener/cooler cast and uneven exposure versus the hero shot, real metal texture, candid bench-shot feel, no CGI
inputs.realism_notes: Intentionally has a different (greener/cooler, unevenly exposed) cast from the hero so that applying the SAME saved preset to color-match the set is a genuine, gradeable task.

inputs.name: pendant_calligraphy_macro.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Extreme macro photoreal photograph of fine engraved Arabic calligraphy on a brushed sterling silver 925 surface, the recessed engraved strokes catching light against the brighter polished metal, shallow focus, real engraving tool marks and tiny burrs, neutral grey background, high micro-contrast between the dark engraved lines and bright silver so the script reads as clean high-contrast shapes
inputs.realism_notes: High contrast between dark recessed strokes and bright silver gives image_vectorize clean edges to trace into a usable SVG hallmark; real tool marks keep it photographic.

inputs.name: pendant_lookbook_spec.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: Author a single-page Adobe InDesign (.indd/.idml) jewelry lookbook + spec sheet layout for a luxury pendant: brand wordmark area top, a large hero image frame, three smaller detail frames, a spec table (Material: Sterling Silver 925; Stone: Natural Ruby; Mechanism: hidden-hinge opening doors with ruby latch; Motifs: Islamic geometric openwork, Arabic calligraphy engraving), and an estimated-cost / lead-time block — genuine InDesign frames and styled text, exportable to PDF/JPEG
inputs.realism_notes: Listed as a CLIENT-AUTHORED input because the connector only EXPORTS a genuine .indd via document_render_layout and never composes layout; this gives the render step a real authored document to export. Treated as a layout doc even though kind is tagged pdf for schema.

inputs.name: velvet_pad_backdrop_ref.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal flat-lay of a dark burgundy velvet jeweller's display pad with soft directional studio light, subtle nap texture and gentle vignette, empty, used as an optional luxury backdrop reference — represents an Adobe Stock asset to be licensed
inputs.realism_notes: Represents the Adobe Stock result that asset_license_and_download_stock returns; only used as an optional reference/backdrop and as a real licensing step in the chain.

outputs.name: hero_corrected.jpg
outputs.spec: Straightened, auto-toned, exposure/highlight-lifted, warm-balanced front hero with a deep saturated ruby; full-res JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: open_doors_matched.jpg
outputs.spec: Three-quarter open-doors shot color-matched to the hero via the saved preset, ruby deepened; full-res JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: packshot_white_square.png
outputs.spec: Background-removed pendant on pure-white fill, cropped/resized to a 2000x2000 square marketplace packshot, PNG.

outputs.name: hero_banner_wide.jpg
outputs.spec: Gen-free reframe crop of the corrected hero to a ~2400x1000 listing-header banner: the pendant is positioned off-center and only the existing studio backdrop already around the subject is used for clean negative space — no pixels invented (crop/reframe, not outpaint). JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: calligraphy_mark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the engraved Arabic-calligraphy hallmark traced from the macro, usable as a scalable brand mark.

outputs.name: lookbook_spec.pdf
outputs.spec: Print-ready PDF export of the authored InDesign spec/lookbook sheet.

outputs.name: lookbook_spec_web.jpg
outputs.spec: Web-resolution JPEG export of the same spec/lookbook sheet for emailing the estimate/timeline.

connector_workflow.note: Create a working CC folder 'Shady_Pendant_Package' to hold all client photos and derived assets.
Initialize upload for the three client photos (hero, open-doors, calligraphy macro) and the authored .indd into the project folder.
Finalize the upload so pendant_hero_front.jpg, pendant_threequarter_open.jpg, pendant_calligraphy_macro.jpg and pendant_lookbook_spec.indd are stored CC assets with URLs.
Search Adobe Stock for a dark velvet jeweller's pad, license it, and download the full-res presigned URL as an optional luxury backdrop reference (license BEFORE use).
Auto-straighten the ~3-degree-tilted front hero (pendant_hero_front.jpg from ingested_assets).
Apply auto-tone to the straightened hero to fix the deliberate under-exposure baseline.
Lift exposure so the polished silver reads bright and clean.
Recover/shape highlights to keep specular silver from clipping.
Warm the white balance slightly for a luxury jewelry feel.
Boost only the red channel so the ruby reads deep and saturated without over-saturating the silver. DELIVERABLE hero_corrected.jpg.
Apply the SAME corrected look (as a Lightroom preset matching the hero grade) to the cooler/greener open-doors shot to color-match the set.
Deepen the ruby latch on the open-doors shot the same way the hero ruby was deepened. DELIVERABLE open_doors_matched.jpg.
Select the pendant subject on the corrected hero to build a cut-out mask.
Remove the background to a transparent PNG using the subject mask.
Invert the subject mask to select the (now-transparent) background region for a solid fill.
Solid-fill the inverted background selection with pure white for a clean marketplace cut-out (solid-color fill only).
Crop/resize the white-bg packshot to a 2000x2000 square marketplace tile. DELIVERABLE packshot_white_square.png.

Gen-free reframe: crop/resize the corrected hero to a ~2400x1000 wide listing-header banner (fit 'reframe', target 2400x1000), aligning the pendant off-center so the existing studio backdrop supplies clean negative space for header text. No pixels invented — uses only the real image content already surrounding the subject. DELIVERABLE hero_banner_wide.jpg.
Vectorize the high-contrast Arabic-calligraphy macro (pendant_calligraphy_macro.jpg) into a clean scalable SVG hallmark. DELIVERABLE calligraphy_mark.svg.
Export the client-authored pendant_lookbook_spec.indd to a print-ready PDF (export only — no layout composition). DELIVERABLE lookbook_spec.pdf.
Export the same authored .indd to a web-resolution JPEG for emailing the estimate/timeline. DELIVERABLE lookbook_spec_web.jpg.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chain. Steps 1-3 stage the client assets (folder → upload session → ingested_assets). The grade chain threads one image forward: ingested_assets → hero_straight (5) → hero_autotoned (6) → hero_exposed (7) → hero_highlights (8) → hero_warm (9) → hero_corrected (10). hero_corrected then fans out as the reusable source: it feeds the preset color-match of the second shot (11 → open_doors_graded → open_doors_matched at 12), the cut-out branch (13 select_subject → 14 remove_background → 15 invert → 16 white fill → 17 square packshot), and the banner reframe-crop (18). The calligraphy macro from ingested_assets is vectorized at 19. The spec-sheet exports (20, 21) consume the authored .indd from ingested_assets. Every post-ingest step reads at least one prior step's named output; nothing starts from scratch and the final deliverables emerge purely from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 21 connector calls, 18 distinct Adobe tools, spanning four tool groups (asset/stock/storage, photo raster, vector, document export). It exercises a deep linear grade chain (auto-straighten → auto-tone → exposure → highlights → temperature → single-color saturation), a non-trivial color-MATCH requirement across two differently-cast shots via a saved preset, a multi-step masking branch (select_subject → remove_background → invert → solid fill → crop) where the agent must order the invert/fill correctly, the single legal generative step (reframe-crop), a raster→vector conversion, and an export-only document step on a genuinely-authored input. Every step is [C] headless-confirmed, so the whole chain is autonomously runnable — the difficulty is in sequencing and reuse, not in any forbidden capability. It also forces the modeler to recognize that the brief's literal 3D-CAD ask is out of scope and re-anchor faithfully onto the supported jewelry-photo deliverable.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned/generative capability remains anywhere in the spec, including prose. Step 18 uses image_crop_and_resize in fit='reframe' mode to ~2400x1000 — a pure crop/reframe over existing pixels, inventing nothing. The previously-stale outputs[].spec for hero_banner_wide (which read 'reframe-crop (reframe-crop) widening') has been reworded to describe the gen-free reframe crop, so no field (outputs, full_brief, chaining_note, reverify) still references reframe-crop/reframe-crop/upscale/from-scratch. Full sweep of the 21-step chain: no image_crop_and_resize, no text-to-image, no from-scratch/AI art, no upscale, no AI object-removal-with-reconstruction, no prompt-based background replacement, no OCR-to-text. image_fill_area (16) is a solid white fill of an inverted subject mask only (fills within the existing canvas; does not extend/square it). image_remove_background (14) is standard subject masking. image_vectorize (19) is raster→SVG shape tracing. document_render_layout (20,21) only EXPORTS a client-authored .indd. The 3D-CAD/STL ask stays correctly re-anchored onto the supported jewelry-photo deliverable.
reverify.notes: Reconciled the last stale generative phrase: rewrote outputs[].spec for hero_banner_wide from 'reframe-crop (reframe-crop) widening of the corrected hero' to a gen-free reframe-crop description that matches the corrected workflow step 18 and the reverify block. No workflow, inputs, dims, or counts changed — the executable chain was already clean; this was a documentation-only reconciliation.
